# .env Doctor

A Python MCP server and web UI that diagnoses .env files for common issues that cause production incidents and security breaches.

## Purpose

Developers frequently ship bugs caused by missing environment variables, weak secrets, misconfigured values, or accidentally committed API keys. .env Doctor catches all of these automatically before they become problems.

## MCP Tools

- `diagnose_env(env_content, example_content)` — Diagnose a .env file passed as a string. Returns a JSON report with score, issues, warnings, and info.
- `diagnose_env_file(env_path, example_path)` — Same but reads from disk paths.
- `scan_project(project_path)` — Scans an entire project directory for all .env variants and returns a combined report.
- `generate_example(env_content)` — Generates a safe .env.example from a real .env, replacing all secrets with placeholders.

## What it detects

- Missing variables: keys in .env.example that don't exist in .env
- Empty values: variables with no value that may cause runtime crashes
- Exposed real secrets: detects patterns matching real API keys (OpenAI sk-, Stripe pk_live_, AWS AKIA, GitHub ghp_, Slack xoxb-, JWTs)
- Weak secrets: placeholder values like "secret", "password", "123456", "changeme" on sensitive fields
- Format errors: URLs not starting with http://, non-numeric ports, non-boolean debug flags, invalid emails
- Undocumented vars: keys in .env not present in .env.example

## Health Score

Each file gets a score 0-100. Critical issues deduct 30 points, errors deduct 10, warnings deduct 5.

## Supported .env variants

.env, .env.local, .env.development, .env.production, .env.staging, .env.test, .env.dev, .env.prod

## Server

Located at server/server.py. Built with FastMCP (Python). Run with: python server/server.py

## Frontend

Located at frontend/index.html. Pure HTML/CSS/JS — no build step, no dependencies. Open directly in browser.

## License

MIT
